title: Display the item link string on an item
excerpt: Is there a way to automatically display the item link string, ie https://domainname.monday.com/boards/999999999999/pulses/1234567890, on an item without copying it manually into a field?

posts.author: PHD (Patty)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there a way to automatically display the item link string, ie https://domainname.monday.com/boards/999999999999/pulses/1234567890, on an item without copying it manually into a field?

posts.author: JHconsultancy (Joseph Honig)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

There isn’t a native way to obtain the complete URL of an item in Monday.com. However, you may consider utilizing the Item ID column and choosing the Item link option as a potential workaround to address your concern.

This approach enables you to generate a clickable hyperlink to the item with the Item ID column alone, eliminating the need to manually copy and paste the URL. This may be a useful alternative, especially if you frequently need to access a specific item and want to avoid typing the URL each time.

Hope this helps

posts.author: PHD (Patty)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you! I didn’t dig deep enough into the native functionality. This totally works for this purpose.

posts.author: KSP (Kelly L Simon)
posts.cookedHtml: I also just need the item link to display or export instead of the item name. The workaround doesn’t work for what I need.

posts.author: YSteinberg (Yoel S)
posts.cookedHtml: All items in one board always have the same URL followed by the item ID, So for example you can have a formula column that concatenates the bords URL (just copy from your browser) with the item ID (using the item ID column as @JHconsultancy pointed out) and then you have the full URL. in my use case I was using a workflow and needed it to export the link, and workflows have “itet ID” as a field, so i just used the boards URL + Item ID and that gets you the exact link
